name: Write Try-Catch-Finally First
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 7: Error Handling
problem: Tests and code written without upfront exception handling leave the program in an undefined state when exceptions occur, and the happy-path logic ends up entangled with error-recovery logic added later.
steps: Write a failing test that forces the exception scenario first.
Write the try-catch-finally structure before filling in the happy-path implementation.
Ensure the catch clause leaves the program in a consistent, expected state.
Add the happy-path implementation inside the try block.
Refactor once tests pass, keeping the try-catch-finally as the outermost concern.
